assertion_results.text: The answer recommends writing a failing test first and clearly references the TDD cycle or red-green-refactor order
assertion_results.evidence: "Follow a tight Red–Green–Refactor TDD loop." and "4) RED: write the smallest failing test" and "Run the suite and confirm it fails for the right reason"

assertion_results.text: The answer says to write only the minimal implementation needed to make the test pass before refactoring
assertion_results.evidence: "5) GREEN: implement the minimum to pass" and "Write the least code needed to make that test pass" followed by "6) REFACTOR: improve design with safety"

assertion_results.text: The answer stays focused on workflow order instead of expanding into general tooling setup
assertion_results.evidence: It expands into tooling and extras: "9) Hardening and quality gates - Type hints + mypy/pyright - Lint/format (ruff/flake8, black) - Consider mutation testing (mutmut/cosmic-ray) ..." and "8) Add property-based checks (optional ...) - Use Hypothesis ..." which go beyond workflow order.
